reasoning: While 'no outside firewood' is a standard rule for pest control, the enforcement here is explicitly retaliatory and selective. A reasonable person would immediately agree that targeting a guest for a service complaint while allowing neighbors to burn prohibited construction waste constitutes an unjust application of the rule.

The application of the rule creates a logical impossibility (Catch-22): the user is required to remove items to regain access, but is denied access until the items are removed. No reasonable person would defend an enforcement mechanism that physically prevents the user from complying with the demand.
